title: passiflora suberosa var. hederacea
intro: Passiflora suberosa var. hederacea, also known as wild passionflower, is a type of flowering vine native to South America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and sedative effects. The health benefits of Passiflora suberosa var. hederacea include relief from anxiety, insomnia, and menstrual cramps, as well as its use in treating digestive issues and as a pain reliever. Herbal preparations of Passiflora suberosa var. hederacea can be found in teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
